steps.title: Find the negative effect value
steps.content: The passage asks for “a neighboring species with a negative effect value.” The signal is clear: scan the “Effect value” column for a minus sign, where that plant appears in the “Neighboring species” column (not as a target).

steps.title: Match the data to the requirement
steps.content:
Common dandelion appears as a neighboring species with an effect value of −0.6254, a negative number.

Himalayan balsam (0.7905) and Virginia spring beauty (0.4853) both show positive values in the table, and star chickweed appears only as a target species, not a neighboring one.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
